text:
There was once a farmer who loved hummingbirds. Every spring he made a special feeder just for them. Every afternoon, he'd sit on his porch swing, gently humming.

One day, a baby hummingbird flew into the feeder. It tipped over, scattering berries everywhere. The farmer looked after it. They became pals.

Eventually, the baby hummingbird flew away. The farmer named it Sparkle.

From time to time, Sparkle visited.

Once Sparkle brought a dozen bright red seeds. The farmer spread them all around.

In springtime each year, the farmer could hear the hummingbirds flitting from seed to seed. They were just like Sparkle.

And the farmer? He was just like a big old hummingbird—all wings and warmth.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
